transcript.text: What's going on, sir?
Hey, Marcel, how you doing?
Really good, man.
I am driving, that's why I'm off camera, but yeah, I'm going to San Francisco for next GSConf tomorrow, and yeah, CPI on Thursday.
Nice, nice, man.
That's awesome.
Do you want to maybe, like, plan to connect live, then, on Thursday?
I am coming, I think, to your CMO breakfast.
I don't know how much I'll be able to stay, because I have to go to that other conference after, but yeah, up to you, whatever works best.
Like, either we can chat quickly now, or...
Yeah, let's do both.
Yeah, that sounds good.
Let's do both, I think, like, would...
I think the way I usually like to start these...
I've been trying to do a lot of them as well, and it's a way to get feedback and sync up and things like that.
We're trying to measure account health based on four vectors, quality of the work product that we're delivering to you, output, and the volume given in relation to the alternative.
Meaning, like, if you were doing this with another vendor or in-house and spending a comparable amount of money on that, like, how much output would you get?

And, you know, and then the quality of output that we're delivering, and then performance, like, the actual results that that's driving for you based on your expectations, and then overall relationship, how much your team and you enjoy working with us and, you know, want to continue to work with us, you know?
So then the way we're doing is, like, one to ten, just to start, and then we'll dig in.
One being complete garbage, couldn't be worse, ten being best possible.

And so maybe we can start with, like, just going one by one really quickly just with the score, and then we'll dig in, you know, but maybe starting with quality, like, from one to ten, how do you feel the quality of the work product that we're delivering?
Yeah, give, like, seven or eight.
We've had some issues that, like, I think the team is catching.
So it's, and we also had, I think, a lot of changes in terms of account management.
So I think, like, there's a little bit of a learning curve for each folks.
So, yeah, I'd say, like, yeah, seven or eight, depending on the week.
Yeah, exactly.
Depending on how good of a day we're having.
Yeah, exactly.
That's funny.
What about volume of output?
I think volume's been pretty consistent.
Yeah, I'd say, like, yeah, I'd like, a nine.
Cool.
And then...
Performance is probably like, yeah, it's seven or eight.
think like we've seen growth over time, but I feel like it's, I don't know if it's, I don't know what it is.
I feel like he hasn't, you it's been a year and so, I don't know, maybe my expectation was too high.
But in terms of like the volume, I was expecting a bit more.
But then again, like you have to put things into context, right?
Like with the whole world shifting to, away from Google and into GEO.
So like, you know, I don't know how much of it is macroeconomic or like, you know, macro level changes versus like growthx performance.
That makes sense.
Yeah.
Like one, one little anecdotal thing is.
Um, I've been doing a lot of, um, like, just overall, like, research for CMSs for my own and things like that.
And, um, but then also, like, Webflow is a customer, but we don't work on the kind of content that's similar to the content we do for you.
And another agency called Graphite works on that kind of content for Webflow.
And I, I've heard from multiple people from Webflow that they're like, oh, and, and brought up strapi specifically.
And a lot of the searches they wish they could come up for, because they're going after developers more, uh, they're like, strapi comes up quite a bit.
I know there are customer reviewers, like, and it's like, yeah, you're, um, and so, like, for instance, I just search, uh, in AI overview or AI mode for Bass headless CMSs for Next.js.
For example, since you're going to the Next.js conference and you are both mentioned and cited there and that, um, you know, in, um.

And so it's like, I think there's a lot of good stuff there, but you're right, like, I think there's even more we can probably do to try to make sure, like, we're optimizing and we're actually launching an AI visibility platform that's going to be open that will hopefully also help to correlate the work we're doing with the impact it's having on your AI visibility as well as, like, LLM referrals, you know.
Yeah, that sounds good.
Yeah, it's funny because, you know, Josh is also the partner at Hypergrove, so we do see, you know, Webpro, obviously, as a competitor.
But, yeah, no, overall, like, you know, like, some of the work that we do, like, I think, like, page that comes up is, like, our Next.js landing page, which was, we predest growthx, right?
So, like, these wheels are, like, trying to measure.

What DevRel is doing internally versus the contribution of ProSax is bringing, and I think, to me, there is some quicker wins that I'm trying to get to, which is making sure we get the fundamentals right, making sure we have the right metas, and making sure we have FAQ on all pages.
And I think that's what's, having those fundamentals right, I think it's what drives success in the long run.

I think the last one is just relationship, and that's mostly 1 to 10 on, like, the team that's currently you're working with.
You know, and then it's overall just...
Like, you know, your level for both you and the team, how much you enjoy working with the team you have, essentially.

Like, think Vivek and Mara, they're doing a great job.
Yeah, overall, like, you know, we've had a lot of changes, so that was a challenge in and out of itself.
But at the same time, like, we got, I feel like we got a stronger team.

Correct.

We'd love to hear more.

the main thing is, like, there's, until, like, like, now, the learning curve is pretty high because, like, you need to get the artifacts right.

so much.

I do.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Review account health and address Strapi's feedback on GrowthX services.](https://fathom.video/share/e46uXATXrqNmHPxGvUsDxFgjZRkhU8ED?tab=summary&timestamp=55.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Performance Gap:** Strapi's growth is below expectations, with the Atlas platform's lack of self-serve capabilities creating workflow bottlenecks.](https://fathom.video/share/e46uXATXrqNmHPxGvUsDxFgjZRkhU8ED?tab=summary&timestamp=469.0)
  - [**Pricing Mismatch:** Strapi's legacy pricing ($230/mo) is misaligned with the current average contract ($18k/mo), causing its custom engineering requests to be de-prioritized.](https://fathom.video/share/e46uXATXrqNmHPxGvUsDxFgjZRkhU8ED?tab=summary&timestamp=967.0)
  - [**Solution → Open Source:** GrowthX will open-source its workflow engine as "ContentOS" to enable Strapi's self-serve needs and align with its open-source DNA.](https://fathom.video/share/e46uXATXrqNmHPxGvUsDxFgjZRkhU8ED?tab=summary&timestamp=1213.0)
  - [**Partnership Opportunity:** Strapi will beta-test ContentOS and support its launch, with a potential strategic investment and legal collaboration on the open-source license.](https://fathom.video/share/e46uXATXrqNmHPxGvUsDxFgjZRkhU8ED?tab=summary&timestamp=1395.0)

## Topics

### Account Health Review

[Marcel's 4-vector health check revealed mixed results:](https://fathom.video/share/e46uXATXrqNmHPxGvUsDxFgjZRkhU8ED?tab=summary&timestamp=68.0)

  - [**Quality:** 7–8/10](https://fathom.video/share/e46uXATXrqNmHPxGvUsDxFgjZRkhU8ED?tab=summary&timestamp=120.0)
      - [**Rationale:** Recent account management changes created a learning curve, but the current team (Vivek, Mara) is strong.](https://fathom.video/share/e46uXATXrqNmHPxGvUsDxFgjZRkhU8ED?tab=summary&timestamp=120.0)
  - [**Output Volume:** 9/10](https://fathom.video/share/e46uXATXrqNmHPxGvUsDxFgjZRkhU8ED?tab=summary&timestamp=168.0)
      - [**Rationale:** Consistent and high.](https://fathom.video/share/e46uXATXrqNmHPxGvUsDxFgjZRkhU8ED?tab=summary&timestamp=168.0)
  - [**Performance:** 7–8/10](https://fathom.video/share/e46uXATXrqNmHPxGvUsDxFgjZRkhU8ED?tab=summary&timestamp=183.0)
      - [**Rationale:** Growth is slower than expected after one year, though macro factors (e.g., Google's shift to SGE) may be a cause.](https://fathom.video/share/e46uXATXrqNmHPxGvUsDxFgjZRkhU8ED?tab=summary&timestamp=183.0)
  - [**Relationship:** 9/10](https://fathom.video/share/e46uXATXrqNmHPxGvUsDxFgjZRkhU8ED?tab=summary&timestamp=410.0)
      - [**Rationale:** The current team is a significant improvement.](https://fathom.video/share/e46uXATXrqNmHPxGvUsDxFgjZRkhU8ED?tab=summary&timestamp=410.0)

### Core Problem: Atlas Bottlenecks & Pricing Mismatch

  - [**Workflow Bottlenecks:** The Atlas platform lacks the self-serve capabilities of its predecessor, AirOps, creating dependencies on GrowthX's engineering team that slow down projects.](https://fathom.video/share/e46uXATXrqNmHPxGvUsDxFgjZRkhU8ED?tab=summary&timestamp=469.0)
      - [**Example:** An image generation project took \~6 months for a "fine" but not "mind-blowing" result.](https://fathom.video/share/e46uXATXrqNmHPxGvUsDxFgjZRkhU8ED?tab=summary&timestamp=535.0)
  - [**Pricing Mismatch:** Strapi's legacy pricing ($230/mo) is a fraction of the current average contract ($18k/mo).](https://fathom.video/share/e46uXATXrqNmHPxGvUsDxFgjZRkhU8ED?tab=summary&timestamp=967.0)
      - [**Impact:** This forces GrowthX to de-prioritize Strapi's custom engineering requests, as they are not scalable across the customer base.](https://fathom.video/share/e46uXATXrqNmHPxGvUsDxFgjZRkhU8ED?tab=summary&timestamp=967.0)

### Solution: Open-Sourcing the Workflow Engine

  - [**The Plan:** GrowthX will open-source its core workflow engine as "ContentOS" to enable Strapi's self-serve needs.](https://fathom.video/share/e46uXATXrqNmHPxGvUsDxFgjZRkhU8ED?tab=summary&timestamp=1213.0)
  - [**Technical Details:**](https://fathom.video/share/e46uXATXrqNmHPxGvUsDxFgjZRkhU8ED?tab=summary&timestamp=1270.0)
      - [**Architecture:** Workflows as code, hosted in GitHub repos owned by the customer.](https://fathom.video/share/e46uXATXrqNmHPxGvUsDxFgjZRkhU8ED?tab=summary&timestamp=1270.0)
      - [**Runtime:** Customers can run workflows on their own infrastructure or use GrowthX's cloud-hosted runtime.](https://fathom.video/share/e46uXATXrqNmHPxGvUsDxFgjZRkhU8ED?tab=summary&timestamp=1315.0)
      - [**Cost Efficiency:** Avoids the high costs of platforms like AirOps (e.g., a single article could cost $1k in credits) by allowing customers to use their own API keys.](https://fathom.video/share/e46uXATXrqNmHPxGvUsDxFgjZRkhU8ED?tab=summary&timestamp=1213.0)
  - [**Timeline:** Self-serve access for Strapi is expected within one month.](https://fathom.video/share/e46uXATXrqNmHPxGvUsDxFgjZRkhU8ED?tab=summary&timestamp=1093.0)

### Partnership & Collaboration

  - [**Beta Testing:** Strapi will be an early beta tester for ContentOS.](https://fathom.video/share/e46uXATXrqNmHPxGvUsDxFgjZRkhU8ED?tab=summary&timestamp=1093.0)
  - [**Launch Support:** Strapi will help amplify the public launch of ContentOS.](https://fathom.video/share/e46uXATXrqNmHPxGvUsDxFgjZRkhU8ED?tab=summary&timestamp=1395.0)
  - [**Legal Collaboration:** GrowthX needs open-source licensing expertise.](https://fathom.video/share/e46uXATXrqNmHPxGvUsDxFgjZRkhU8ED?tab=summary&timestamp=1504.0)
      - [**Action:** Victor will connect GrowthX with JJ from OSS Capital.](https://fathom.video/share/e46uXATXrqNmHPxGvUsDxFgjZRkhU8ED?tab=summary&timestamp=1504.0)
  - [**Strategic Investment:** Marcel mentioned a recent strategic investment from HubSpot and suggested Strapi could consider a similar, smaller investment to deepen the partnership.](https://fathom.video/share/e46uXATXrqNmHPxGvUsDxFgjZRkhU8ED?tab=summary&timestamp=1440.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Marcel:**](https://fathom.video/share/e46uXATXrqNmHPxGvUsDxFgjZRkhU8ED?tab=summary&timestamp=1093.0)
      - [Provide Strapi with self-serve access to ContentOS within one month.](https://fathom.video/share/e46uXATXrqNmHPxGvUsDxFgjZRkhU8ED?tab=summary&timestamp=1093.0)
      - [Schedule a demo of ContentOS for Victor at the Thursday breakfast.](https://fathom.video/share/e46uXATXrqNmHPxGvUsDxFgjZRkhU8ED?tab=summary&timestamp=1602.0)
      - [Have Bridget (GrowthX) contact Victor for an introduction to OSS Capital.](https://fathom.video/share/e46uXATXrqNmHPxGvUsDxFgjZRkhU8ED?tab=summary&timestamp=1504.0)
  - [**Victor:**](https://fathom.video/share/e46uXATXrqNmHPxGvUsDxFgjZRkhU8ED?tab=summary&timestamp=1504.0)
      - [Connect Bridget (GrowthX) with JJ from OSS Capital.](https://fathom.video/share/e46uXATXrqNmHPxGvUsDxFgjZRkhU8ED?tab=summary&timestamp=1504.0)

action_items.description: Add Strapi to ContentOS beta; notify Victor when ready
Have Bridget email Victor re: OSS/IP; request intros (JJ/Heather)
